column1: The island's native tribe has grown accustomed to these recent occurrences of dense fog, even though it appears (and dissipates) quite rapidly.
column2: 島上的原住民部落已經習慣了最近這陣濃霧，儘管它來得快、去得也快。

column1: What the natives don't know, however, is that the fog is just a smokescreen to cover the activities of an evil force so sinister, so sly, so slick, that the mere mention of his name brings fear to the heart of the staunchest man...
column2: 然而島民不知道的是，這陣濃霧其實只是煙幕，用來掩護一股邪惡勢力的行動——這股勢力如此陰險、狡詐、狠毒，光是提起他的名字，就足以讓最勇敢的漢子也心生畏懼……

column1: NONOOKEE!
column2: 諾努基！

column1: Inside his volcanic mountain fortress, the dirty Doctor is designing the most disgusting of his dastardly deeds.
column2: 在他的火山堡壘深處，這個齷齪的博士正在策劃他最卑鄙下流的邪惡計畫。

column1: Say, aren't you that guy that won that big lottery prize? asks the pilot.
column2: 「欸，你是不是就是中了樂透大獎的那個人？」飛行員問道。

column1: Yep, that's me, you reply proudly. %s
column2: 「沒錯，就是我，」你得意地回答。%s

column1: Well, Larry, I've got a little surprise for you. I just heard on the radio: they just went bankrupt. Kaput. Busted. I hope you saved some of that money.
column2: 「賴瑞啊，我有個小驚喜要告訴你。我剛剛聽廣播說，他們破產了。完蛋了，倒了。希望你有把那筆錢存下來一些。」

column1: It doesn't matter, you assert, "I have the love of a beautiful woman and we can live here on this wonderful island for the rest of our lives. Why do I need money? I'll give up my leisure suit for a muumuu!"
column2: 「這沒關係，」你堅定地說，「我有一個美麗女人的愛，我們可以在這座美好的島上共度餘生。我要錢幹嘛？我願意脫下休閒西裝，換上一件夏威夷長袍！」

column1: Where is that woman? Why is she so slow? shouts Dr. Nonookee. "She should be here by now!"
column2: 「那個女人到底在哪裡？怎麼這麼慢？」諾努基博士吼道，「她現在應該已經到了！」

column1: Sir, crackles the radio, "shipment incoming!"
column2: 「長官，」無線電傳來沙沙聲，「貨物即將送達！」

column1: Ah, ha! Here she is. That's more like it! he says.
column2: 「啊哈！她來了，這才像話！」他說道。

column1: Calling LA. Calling LA. he says into the microphone.
column2: 「呼叫LA，呼叫LA，」他對著麥克風說道。

column1: Yes, sir, responds a female voice on the radio.
column2: 「是的，長官，」無線電裡傳來一個女聲回應。

column1: Is everything in place for the transfer? he asks.
column2: 「轉移的一切都準備好了嗎？」他問道。

column1: Everything! she snaps back.
column2: 「一切就緒！」她厲聲回答。

column1: He smiles a broad smile.
column2: 他咧嘴一笑。

column1: Excellent, LA. Keep me informed, he concludes, "and remember: no mistakes!"
column2: 「很好，LA，隨時向我回報，」他總結道，「記住，不准出任何差錯！」

column1: Evidently the bad Doctor is planning something to do with Los Angeles and some sort of transfer. What could it be?
column2: 顯然這個壞博士正在策劃某件跟洛杉磯還有某種「轉移」有關的事。到底會是什麼呢？

column1: FAN ME, he shouts, "AND NOW!"
column2: 「給我搧風，」他吼道，「現在馬上！」

column1: Now, feed me grapes, he orders with a sinister smile, "and keep 'em coming!"
column2: 「現在，餵我吃葡萄，」他帶著陰險的笑容命令道，「不准停！」

column1: Gosh, Larry; let's hope you never end up on the bad side of this character!
column2: 老天，賴瑞，希望你永遠別惹到這個角色！

column1: You are so impressed! You've never had a girl invite you to her pad before -- not to mention her landing pad!
column2: 你佩服得五體投地！以前從沒有女生邀你去她的「小窩」——更別說是她的「停機坪」了！

column1: Hey, baby, you say, "What a great, kinky bed!"
column2: 「嘿，寶貝，」你說，「這張床真是又讚又騷！」

column1: Hey! What's going on here? you shout. "Get these ankle cuffs off me! And, why the handcuffs?"
column2: 「嘿！這是怎麼回事？」你大喊，「把我腳上的鐐銬拿掉！還有，為什麼要銬手銬？」

column1: Oh, I'm just into mechanical devices, she replies with a smile. "Hold still, darling, this will only take a second."
column2: 「噢，我就是喜歡機械小玩意兒，」她微笑著回答，「別動，親愛的，一下子就好。」

column1: Why does that look like hydrofluoric acid below me? you shout in terror.
column2: 「為什麼我下面看起來像是氫氟酸？」你驚恐地大叫。

column1: Because it is, silly, she responds.
column2: 「因為那就是氫氟酸啊，笨蛋，」她回答。

column1: Well, Larry; this brings a whole new meaning to the term "dismemberment!"
column2: 賴瑞啊，這下「肢解」這個詞對你來說有了全新的意義！

column1: You just wait over there on the bed, while I slip something, she says with a smile.
column2: 「你就在床上等一下，我去換件衣服，」她微笑著說。

column1: Perhaps if things go well tonight, you think to yourself, "I could give her a subscription to 'Popular Mechanics!'"
column2: 「要是今晚一切順利，」你心裡想著，「或許我可以送她一份《大眾機械》雜誌訂閱！」

column1: You are quickly taken to a local office of the KGB where a specialist in onklunk extraction is busy giving you the third degree!
column2: 你很快就被押到當地的KGB辦公室，一位「昂克隆克萃取」專家正忙著對你嚴刑拷問！

column1: And so, my leettle white-suited capitalist swine, says the KGB agent, "you vill now tell us zee location of zat onklunk or I vill be forced to run zeese alto saxophone reeds under your fingernails until you're zinging like zee Bird!"
column2: 「那麼，我的小白西裝資本主義走狗，」KGB幹員說，「你現在就給偶說出那個昂克隆克藏在哪裡，不然偶就要拿中音薩克斯風簧片插進你的指甲縫，插到你像『Bird』那樣鬼哭神號為止！」

column1: Things don't look good, Larry. Maybe next time a different approach would be better...
column2: 情況不妙啊，賴瑞。或許下次換個方式會比較好……

column1: Attention QA: this machine has an Al Lowe speed rating of %d. Please write down the number and the type of machine you're on.
column2: 注意QA人員：這台機器的艾爾·洛威速度評分為%d。請記下這個數字以及你使用的機器型號。

column1: (Note: your mileage may vary.)
column2: （註：實際結果因人而異。）

column1: Every door here is locked, except the doors leading back to the street. There are signs over the doors.
column2: 這裡的每一扇門都上了鎖，除了通往街上的那扇。門上都掛著告示牌。

column1: It's just a modern lighting fixture, not a trapeze upon which she performs!
column2: 那只是個現代風的燈具，不是她拿來表演空中飛人的鞦韆！

column1: The sign over the west door says "The Dating Connection, CLOSED SET."
column2: 西邊門上的告示寫著「約會連線——攝影棚重地，謝絕進入」。

column1: The sign over the east door says "Lucky Life Lottery, CLOSED SET."
column2: 東邊門上的告示寫著「幸運人生樂透——攝影棚重地，謝絕進入」。

column1: The sign over the north door says "Green Room."
column2: 北邊門上的告示寫著「綠色房間」。

column1: The sign over the south door says "EXIT."
column2: 南邊門上的告示寫著「出口」。

column1: Don't you love modern art.
column2: 你就是喜歡這種現代藝術，對吧。

column1: She has a nice pair of skylights, eh?
column2: 她那對「天窗」還真不賴，是吧？

column1: The television studio lobby is lavishly decorated in the latest trend. A receptionist sits in the center of the room. There are doors in every wall.
column2: 電視台的大廳裝潢得極盡奢華、緊跟時尚潮流。一位接待員坐在房間正中央，四面牆上都有門。

column1: The only door you can open is the door you used to enter from the street.
column2: 你唯一打得開的門，就是你剛才從街上進來的那扇。

column1: Guess there must have been a fast-breaking story somewhere else!
column2: 想必是別的地方有突發新聞要搶先報導吧！

column1: Hello, big, blonde and beautiful, you say. %s
column2: 「哈囉，豐滿的金髮美女，」你說道。%s

column1: Hiya, she blurts between bubbles, "kin I helpya?"
column2: 「嗨呀，」她嚼著口香糖含糊地說，「有什麼可以幫你的嗎？」

column1: Sure you can help me, you tell the girl, "can you get out from behind that counter?"
column2: 「你當然可以幫我，」你對女孩說，「你可以從那個櫃檯後面出來嗎？」

column1: (She ignores your feeble attempt at humor.)
column2: （她無視了你那蹩腳的幽默。）

column1: Say, you ask the receptionist, "is this lottery ticket any good?"
column2: 「請問，」你問接待員，「這張樂透彩券中獎了嗎？」

column1: I don't know, she replies, "I've misplaced my glasses! As best I can remember, this week's Lucky Life Lottery Luck-O Buck-O numbers are: %d, %d, %d, %d, %d, and %d. What six numbers do you have?"
column2: 「我不知道耶，」她回答，「我眼鏡不知道放哪兒去了！我印象中，這禮拜幸運人生樂透的幸運發財號碼是：%d、%d、%d、%d、%d和%d。你的六個號碼是什麼？」

column1: Why, that's correct! she replies excitedly. "You ARE a lucky guy! The last Lucky Life Lottery show of the season is being taped RIGHT NOW! I'll notify the director that you're here; I'm sure you'll be called immediately."
column2: 「哎呀，完全正確！」她興奮地回答，「你真的是個幸運兒！這一季最後一集幸運人生樂透節目現在正在錄影！我會通知導播你來了，我相信你馬上就會被叫進去。」

column1: I'll unlock the door to the green room, so you can wait there.
column2: 「我來幫你把綠色房間的門打開，你可以在那裡等。」

column1: Oh, by the way, says the receptionist, "don't get nervous just because you're on live television and being watched by millions of people!"
column2: 「噢，對了，」接待員說，「別因為你要上現場直播、被幾百萬人盯著看，就緊張兮兮的喔！」

column1: She chuckles softly under her breath.
column2: 她低聲輕笑了一下。

column1: Well, that's too bad, she replies. "I'm sorry, but you cannot get on the big TV show unless you have all six lucky numbers."
column2: 「哎，真可惜，」她回答，「很抱歉，除非你六個幸運號碼都對，不然沒辦法上這個大節目。」

column1: Why don't you try again when you're a winner!
column2: 「等你哪天中獎了再來試試看吧！」

column1: Hey! Was that somebody famous?
column2: 嘿！那該不會是什麼名人吧？

column1: You find dull women sexy.
column2: 你覺得無趣的女人也挺性感的。

column1: You can't. They are all locked. And, there's no key!
column2: 你打不開。全都上了鎖，而且根本沒有鑰匙！

column1: Just be patient. Have a seat. Kick back. Relax.
column2: 耐心點吧，找張椅子坐下，放輕鬆，別急。

column1: That Skirvin guy sure can draw horses!
column2: 那個叫史克文的傢伙，馬畫得真不錯！

column1: There's one door that's open.
column2: 有一扇門是開著的。

column1: (Why aren't you walking through it?)
column2: （你怎麼還不走過去？）

column1: There are doors to the east, west and south, which are all locked. Tight. And, there's no key.
column2: 東邊、西邊、南邊都有門，但全都鎖得死死的，而且沒有鑰匙。

column1: (Looks like you're trapped in here until someone comes to your rescue, Larry!)
column2: （看來你被困在這裡了，賴瑞，得等人來救你！）

column1: One monitor shows a lovely young lady; the other, just static.
column2: 一台監視器畫面上是位漂亮的年輕女子，另一台則只有雜訊。

column1: It looks comfortable.
column2: 看起來挺舒服的。

column1: You wonder why the receptionist called this a green room, when there's no green in it at all! There are two television monitors, a bench, some art posters, a lovely modern painting and three doors.
column2: 你納悶接待員為什麼把這裡叫做「綠色房間」，明明裡面一點綠色都沒有！房裡有兩台電視監視器、一張長凳、幾張藝術海報、一幅漂亮的現代畫，還有三扇門。

column1: Oh, thay, you! Poo Poo, cries the man, "You're late! Where have you been, you thilly little dickenth?! We've justh been worried thick about you."
column2: 「噢，唉唷，你！噗噗，」男人扯著嗓子喊道，帶著點漏風的口音，「你遲到了啦！你跑到哪裡去了，你這個小傻瓜？！我們大家都擔心死了呢。」

column1: Before you can answer him, he tells you: "Hurry up, Honey, you're ON!!"
column2: 你還沒來得及回答，他就對你說：「快點，甜心，你要上場了！！」

column1: The assistant producer looks at you with disdain, "Well, Laffer, I musth admit none of usth in the control room exthpected you to win. I'm thure you're not proud of the outcome, but, ruleths are ruleths I thuppoth."
column2: 副製作人一臉不屑地看著你，「好吧，拉夫先生，我得承認，控制室裡沒人猜到你會贏。我相信你自己也不會太得意這個結果，但規矩就是規矩，沒辦法。」

column1: Here is your cruithe thip ticket. All of usth here at 'The Dating Connecthion' wisth you the besth of luck.
column2: 「這是你的郵輪船票。我們『約會連線』全體工作人員，祝你好運。」

column1: You'll need it!
column2: 「你會需要的！」

column1: Hey, you! cries the woman, "Where have you been? We've been looking all over for you."
column2: 「嘿，你！」女人喊道，「你跑去哪了？我們到處在找你。」

column1: I just won 'The Dating Connection,' you reply.
column2: 「我剛贏了『約會連線』，」你回答。

column1: Big deal. You were supposed to wait here. Now hurry and follow me!
column2: 「了不起喔。你應該在這裡等的。現在快點跟我來！」

column1: (Oh no, Larry. Here we go again!)
column2: （噢不，賴瑞，又來了！）

column1: The control room talkback speaker crackles, "Five seconds to air, boys and girls. Five seconds to air! Places, everybody!"
column2: 控制室的對講喇叭傳來沙沙聲：「還有五秒開播，各位。五秒後開播！各就各位！」

column1: This doesn't look at all like you expected a lottery show to look. But, just in case, you decide to grab a seat on that empty stool over there.
column2: 這裡完全不像你想像中樂透節目該有的樣子。不過為了保險起見，你決定在那邊那張空凳子上坐下。

column1: The voiceover announcer says, "From Hollywood, it's the latest and greatest in embarrassment programming -- the all new 'Dating Connection!'"
column2: 旁白主持人說道：「來自好萊塢，最新、最尷尬的節目——全新『約會連線』！」

column1: And here's your host, Biff Barf!
column2: 「歡迎你們的主持人，比夫·巴夫！」

column1: Thank you and welcome; welcome, everybody! I'm so glad you're here for the all new 'Dating Connection.'
column2: 「謝謝，歡迎，歡迎大家！很高興你們能來看這個全新的『約會連線』。」

column1: We're just about ready to play our game, so let's meet our contestants. Who's going to make their big 'Dating Connection' today?
column2: 「我們馬上就要開始遊戲了，先來認識一下我們的參賽者吧。今天誰會找到他的『約會連線』呢？」

column1: Biff, today's lovely bachelorette is Barbara Bimbo, of Airhead, California.
column2: 「比夫，今天這位可愛的單身女郎是來自加州『空腦鎮』的芭芭拉·賓波。」

column1: Barbara's hobbies are computer programming, creating unusual milkshakes, and tantalizing elderly men. She lists her turn-ons as industrial-grade blenders and RS-232 interfacing.
column2: 「芭芭拉的興趣是寫程式、調配奇怪的奶昔，還有撩撥老先生。她說她的興奮點是工業級果汁機和RS-232串列介面。」

column1: Turn-offs include international military conflicts and the aroma of rosin-core solder.
column2: 「她的雷點包括國際軍事衝突，還有松香焊錫的味道。」

column1: On the left is bachelor number one, a professional surfboard waxer from Gumbo, Missouri. His hobbies include collecting braunschweiger casings, speculating on the sexual preference of professional dancers, and watching televised opera with the sound off.
column2: 「左邊這位是一號男嘉賓，一位來自密蘇里州『秋葵濃湯鎮』的職業衝浪板打蠟師。他的興趣包括收集香腸腸衣、猜測職業舞者的性向，以及把電視歌劇轉靜音看。」

column1: Meet Davie Blair!
column2: 「歡迎戴維·布萊爾！」

column1: Bachelor number two is today's token intellectual. He is presently chairman of the Physics Department at our local university, F.U.
column2: 「二號男嘉賓是今天節目裡的『學術擔當』。他目前是本地大學——去你大學——物理系的系主任。」

column1: Originally from Pakistan, Ohio, meet Raguka Singh Soong.
column2: 「原籍俄亥俄州『巴基斯坦鎮』，歡迎拉古卡·辛格·宋。」

column1: Hey, that's not your name!
column2: 嘿，那才不是你的名字！

column1: Uh, excuse me, you shout. %s
column2: 「呃，不好意思，」你喊道。%s

column1: Ok, Raguka Larry, responds Biff. "Whatever you want to call yourself is ok with me!"
column2: 「好的，拉古卡·賴瑞，」比夫回答，「你想叫自己什麼都行，我沒意見！」

column1: And, on the far right today is bachelor number three, a journalism major who quit college in order to pursue his dream job: fact-checker on the 'National Enquiry' newspaper.
column2: 「而今天最右邊這位是三號男嘉賓，新聞系肄業生，為了追求他的夢幻工作——擔任《國家問詢報》的事實查核員——而輟學。」

column1: Currently unemployed, meet A. P. Wire!
column2: 「目前無業，歡迎A·P·電訊！」

column1: And now, it's time to play 'The Dating Connection!' says Biff Barf. "Barbara, may we have your first question please?"
column2: 「好了，現在是『約會連線』遊戲時間！」比夫·巴夫說道，「芭芭拉，可以請你出第一題了嗎？」

column1: Ok like thanks, Biff, ya know, says Barbara. "Bachelor number one, like this one's for you!"
column2: 「好啊，就是，謝啦，比夫，你知道的，」芭芭拉說，「一號嘉賓，這題，就是要問你的啦！」

column1: If I was to go like out on a you know date with you and you was like you know all dressed up or sumpthin' and like the car busted and we had to walk for help and I like broke a you know heel like how would you fix it?
column2: 「如果我，就是說，跟你出去約會，然後你，你知道的，穿得整整齊齊還是怎樣的，然後車子突然壞掉，我們得走路去找人幫忙，然後我，就是說，把鞋跟弄斷了，你會怎麼幫我修？」
